name: Piece switch: Chopin Nocturne flat dynamics then Bach Prelude solid timing

observations.observation_text: The melodic line in the right hand stays at a constant mezzo-forte -- the melody should float above the accompaniment, so try bringing it out more while keeping the left hand softer.
The climax phrase in bar 12 should be the loudest point in the piece, but it barely differs from the surrounding texture.
Still little dynamic variety across the nocturne -- the pp and mf markings are treated at nearly the same level throughout.
The steady sixteenth-note pulse in the Bach prelude is very solid -- good rhythmic grounding in the new piece.
The arpeggiated figures are clean and evenly voiced -- each note in the broken chord receives equal weight, which works well here.

name: Piece switch: alternating pieces with different pedaling issues

observations.observation_text: In the Moonlight Sonata, the pedal is held too long through the triplet arpeggios, merging the bass octaves into an indistinct wash.
The Reverie calls for a seamless pedal that blurs harmonies intentionally -- but here you are lifting too early and breaking the impressionistic haze.
Still over-pedaling the Moonlight -- the bass note in bar 3 is bleeding into bar 4 and muddying the harmony change.
In the Reverie, the pedal is still releasing too early before the melodic line resolves, leaving phrases sounding disconnected.

expected_facts.gold_fact_text: In Beethoven Moonlight Sonata, student over-pedals through the triplet arpeggios, blurring bass octaves.
In Debussy Reverie, student lifts pedal too early, breaking the intended impressionistic legato.

observations.observation_text: The forte passages in the Beethoven are played too tentatively -- the sf accents in bar 6 should land with more physical conviction.
The Beethoven still lacks the stark contrast between the stormy forte and the lyrical piano passages -- the drama depends on that gulf.
Beautiful shaping in the Debussy -- the gradual swell from pp to mf in bars 4-8 creates a sense of the tide coming in.
The pp entrance at bar 1 of the Debussy is perfectly calibrated -- that stillness sets the whole atmospheric stage.
